actions.label: Hire a Sales Manager
actions.description: Hand off growth to a sales manager who picks and runs your smartest marketing move each month, so you can focus on finance.
actions.lesson: Delegation is the pivot from operator to owner. Once you've learned how to sell and generate leads yourself, you hand the playbook to a manager. They run growth; you graduate to the higher-leverage work of structuring capital.
actions.narrative_success: Your sales manager is in the seat. Each month they pick and execute the smartest growth play, so you can step back from marketing and focus on the money.
actions.narrative_failure: Your sales manager is onboarding. They'll be running your growth moves from here.

actions.label: Sharpen Your Offer
actions.description: Package your expertise into a clear, premium offer so every customer is worth more.
actions.lesson: Sell outcomes, not hours — to people who can pay. A sharper, premium offer raises the value of every single customer you have, which is far more powerful than just adding more of them.
actions.narrative_success: The new pricing feels scary — and every new prospect is saying yes faster than before. You were undercharging.
actions.narrative_failure: Two clients pushed back on the new pricing. You compromised on one. The packaging needs another pass.

actions.label: Build a Content Engine
actions.description: Hire a content team so your brand grows on autopilot, lifting what every customer is worth.
actions.lesson: A content team makes your brand compound on autopilot. A bigger brand means higher perceived value — you raise prices and capacity without trading more of your hours for attention.
actions.narrative_success: The content calendar runs itself. You approved this month's posts in twenty minutes. Your brand — and your pricing power — keeps climbing.
actions.narrative_failure: The team is producing content. It's... fine. Not your voice yet. Takes time to train a brand voice into other people.

actions.label: Cold Outreach (Calls & DMs)
actions.description: Fill your funnel by hand with cold calls, DMs, and in-person networking to put names in the pipeline.
actions.lesson: Early on, volume wins. Talk to enough of the right people — online and in person — and the math produces leads. In-person especially: people remember someone they've met, and those become your warmest leads. Don't polish the funnel before you've filled it.
actions.narrative_success: Dozens of calls and a few handshakes later, your pipeline has more names in it than it's ever had — and a couple of people approached you for once.
actions.narrative_failure: A lot of calls and business cards, one solid maybe. But that's a lead and a few new faces who now know your name.

actions.label: Post on Social Media
actions.description: Post consistently about your work to build an audience that feeds leads over time.
actions.lesson: Organic content compounds slowly but never stops. It won't pay this month — it builds an audience that feeds leads into your funnel for years.
actions.narrative_success: One post went semi-viral in your niche. Your DMs have actual prospects in them for the first time.
actions.narrative_failure: Consistent posting, crickets responding. But the archive is building — it'll compound eventually.

actions.label: Run Paid Ads
actions.description: Run a paid ads test to learn what message lands and turn ad spend into leads fast.
actions.lesson: Master one channel at a time. Paid ads give the fastest feedback and the fastest leads — pour in there first, build organic in parallel for the long game.
actions.narrative_success: The ads are profitable. Not wildly — but the cost per lead is low enough to pour more in. You've found a lever.
actions.narrative_failure: The ad budget mostly evaporated. But you learned what doesn't work, which is worth something.

actions.label: Ask for Referrals
actions.description: Ask every client for warm introductions — the cheapest, highest-trust leads you can get.
actions.lesson: Referrals are the cheapest, highest-trust leads you'll ever get. Happy clients are a sales force you don't pay — but only if you actually ask.
actions.narrative_success: Your best client introduced you to three people at her networking group. Warm leads, already half-sold.
actions.narrative_failure: Everyone said they'd think about it. One person actually came through — better than nothing.

actions.label: Sell It Yourself
actions.description: Close deals personally — your cheapest, highest-converting sales channel early on is you.
actions.lesson: Before you can afford a sales team or a funnel, you are the sales team. Founder-led selling is the cheapest, highest-converting channel you have early on — and every call teaches you what actually makes people buy.
actions.narrative_success: You closed a handful of deals by sheer hustle — calls, follow-ups, and asking for the sale. It's exhausting, but the pipeline turned into paying customers.
actions.narrative_failure: A lot of calls, a few maybes, one yes. Selling is a numbers game and this month the numbers were thin — but you learned what objections to expect.

actions.label: Set Up a Sales Pipeline
actions.description: Build a real sales pipeline with stages and follow-ups so fewer leads slip away.
actions.lesson: What gets tracked gets closed. A real pipeline surfaces the leads you'd otherwise forget and lifts your conversion — most lost sales die from neglect, not rejection.
actions.narrative_success: For the first time, you can see every prospect, where they are, and what happens next. Three forgotten leads turned into deals.
actions.narrative_failure: The CRM is set up but half your data is still in sticky notes. Partial improvement — you'll migrate the rest next month.

actions.label: Celebrity Influencer Megadeal
actions.description: Lock in a one-off shoutout from a million-follower creator — instant reach, your brand in front of a massive audience overnight.
actions.lesson: A borrowed audience rarely converts. You paid a fortune for raw reach from people who aren't your buyer — a spike that vanished in days while support drowned in tire-kickers. Reach isn't pipeline; fit and follow-up are.
actions.narrative_success: The post hit millions — a flood of curiosity clicks, a screenshot-worthy spike, a rush of new leads. Then: crickets. The audience wasn't your buyer, the bump evaporated within a week, and the few who did sign up churned fast.

actions.label: Hire a High-Ticket Closer
actions.description: A smooth, expensive closer swears he'll 10x your sales overnight with high-ticket offers. Big promises, big personality, ~$9k/mo. What could go wrong?
actions.lesson: A great closer can transform revenue — but the wrong one closes with fake guarantees that wreck your brand, gaslight your team, and invite lawsuits. Hiring raw closing skill without character is a classic, costly trap; you'll have to fire him, and he won't go quietly.
actions.narrative_success: He starts Monday and immediately starts closing big-ticket deals — the numbers spike, the pipeline buzzes. He's loud, expensive, and already acting like he owns the place. But hey, revenue's up. For now.
